mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of housing starts and revolving consumer credit signals synchronized real-economy and financial demand growth. When both series rise simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to confirmed aggregate demand strength, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are indistinguishable from baseline when HOUST and RSAFS diverge.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising industrial production reflects capacity utilization normalization rather than inflationary overheating. This supply-side improvement expands profit margins and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during supply-constrained periods, indicating the signal fails under capacity bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized, corporate earnings growth accelerates sufficiently to override valuation compression, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: The signal generates a negative rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth crosses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reduces recession risk premiums and supports equity risk appetite, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows a negative correlation between monthly PAYEMS changes and 5-day SPX returns across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising core inflation and a rising federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that eventually suppresses equity valuations. When both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise concurrently, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days turn negative due to discount rate expansion and margin compression.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, or the interaction term fails to predict negative 60-day returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, housing starts act as a leading indicator of credit cycle peak and subsequent demand contraction. When HOUST declines for two consecutive months in this regime, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days turn negative as corporate earnings face downward revision risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where HOUST falls for two consecutive months during late-cycle phases, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.
